;; Parse infix expression: digits, 'O' (min), 'X' (+), parens, spaces
;; Grammar: E := NUMBER | '(' E op E ')' where op is O or X
(define (parse s)
  (let ((pos 0))
    (define (peek) (if (< pos (string-length s)) (string-ref s pos) #\null))
    (define (advance) (set! pos (+ pos 1)))
    (define (skip-ws) (let loop () (when (and (< pos (string-length s)) (char=? (peek) #\space)) (advance) (loop))))
    (define (parse-num)
      (let loop ((acc '()))
        (if (and (< pos (string-length s)) (char-numeric? (peek)))
            (let ((c (peek))) (advance) (loop (cons c acc)))
            (string->number (list->string (reverse acc))))))
    (define (parse-expr)
      (skip-ws)
      (cond
        ((char=? (peek) #\()
         (advance)
         (let ((left (parse-expr)))
           (skip-ws)
           (let ((op (peek))) (advance)
             (skip-ws)
             (let ((right (parse-expr)))
               (skip-ws)
               (when (char=? (peek) #\)) (advance))
               (if (char=? op #\O) (min left right) (+ left right))))))
        (else (parse-num))))
    (parse-expr)))

(parse expr-str)

;; Parse: expression is either number or (expr OP expr)
(define (parse toks)
  ;; returns (value . rest-tokens)
  (let ((t (car toks)))
    (cond
      ((number? t) (cons t (cdr toks)))
      ((eq? t 'lp)
        (let* ((r1 (parse (cdr toks)))
               (v1 (car r1))
               (rest1 (cdr r1))
               (op (car rest1))
               (r2 (parse (cdr rest1)))
               (v2 (car r2))
               (rest2 (cdr r2)))
          ;; rest2 should start with 'rp
          (if (not (eq? (car rest2) 'rp))
              (error "expected rp")
              (cons
                (if (eq? op 'plus) (min v1 v2) (+ v1 v2))
                (cdr rest2)))))
      (else (error "unexpected" t)))))

(define result (parse tokens))
(car result)

(define (pl a b) (+ a b))

;; Tokenize: numbers, (, ), ⊕, ⊗. Then parse infix -> evaluate.
;; Since every operation is parenthesized as (A op B), we can do a simple recursive parse.

(define (tokenize str)
  (let loop ((i 0) (tokens '()))
    (if (>= i (string-length str))
        (reverse tokens)
        (let ((c (string-ref str i)))
          (cond
           ((char=? c #\space) (loop (+ i 1) tokens))
           ((char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'lp tokens)))
           ((char=? c #\)) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'rp tokens)))
           ((char-numeric? c)
            (let scan ((j i))
              (if (and (< j (string-length str)) (char-numeric? (string-ref str j)))
                  (scan (+ j 1))
                  (loop j (cons (string->number (substring str i j)) tokens)))))
           (else
            ;; multi-byte char for ⊕ or ⊗. In this string, they occupy 3 bytes but 1 char in Scheme.
            ;; Test char codepoint
            (let ((cp (char->integer c)))
              (cond
               ((= cp #x2295) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'plus tokens)))  ; ⊕
               ((= cp #x2297) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'times tokens))) ; ⊗
               (else (error "bad char" c cp))))))))))

(define sample "((39 ⊕ 21) ⊗ 5)")
(tokenize sample)

;; Simple recursive descent parser for fully parenthesized binary expressions.
;; Grammar: E = NUMBER | "(" E OP E ")"  where OP is + or *
;; + means min (tropical ⊕), * means ordinary addition (tropical ⊗)

(define (parse-eval s)
  (let ((i 0)
        (n (string-length s)))
    (define (peek) (if (< i n) (string-ref s i) #f))
    (define (advance) (set! i (+ i 1)))
    (define (skip-ws)
      (let loop ()
        (when (and (< i n) (char=? (string-ref s i) #\space))
          (advance) (loop))))
    (define (parse-number)
      (skip-ws)
      (let loop ((acc 0))
        (let ((c (peek)))
          (if (and c (char-numeric? c))
              (begin (advance) (loop (+ (* acc 10) (- (char->integer c) (char->integer #\0)))))
              acc))))
    (define (parse-expr)
      (skip-ws)
      (if (char=? (peek) #\()
          (begin
            (advance) ; consume (
            (let ((left (parse-expr)))
              (skip-ws)
              (let ((op (peek)))
                (advance) ; consume op
                (let ((right (parse-expr)))
                  (skip-ws)
                  (advance) ; consume )
                  (cond ((char=? op #\+) (min left right))
                        ((char=? op #\*) (+ left right))
                        (else (error "bad op")))))))
          (parse-number)))
    (parse-expr)))

(parse-eval input)

;; Sanity check with small test
(parse-eval "(3 + 5)")  ; should be min(3,5) = 3

(define (str-replace s old new)
  (let loop ((s s) (out ""))
    (let ((idx (string-contains s old)))
      (if idx
          (loop (substring s (+ idx (string-length old)) (string-length s))
                (string-append out (substring s 0 idx) new))
          (string-append out s)))))

;; Tokenize: numbers, parens, and operators ⊕ ⊗
;; Note: ⊕ and ⊗ are multi-byte UTF-8 chars. We'll work on the string char-by-char.

(define (tokenize s)
  (let loop ((i 0) (toks '()))
    (cond
      ((>= i (string-length s)) (reverse toks))
      (else
        (let ((c (string-ref s i)))
          (cond
            ((char=? c #\space) (loop (+ i 1) toks))
            ((char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'lp toks)))
            ((char=? c #\)) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'rp toks)))
            ((and (char>=? c #\0) (char<=? c #\9))
             ;; read number
             (let num-loop ((j i) (digits '()))
               (if (and (< j (string-length s))
                        (let ((cc (string-ref s j)))
                          (and (char>=? cc #\0) (char<=? cc #\9))))
                   (num-loop (+ j 1) (cons (string-ref s j) digits))
                   (loop j (cons (string->number (list->string (reverse digits))) toks)))))
            (else
             ;; assume operator char (multi-byte). Peek character.
             ;; Just check: if we see '⊕' or '⊗'. Use substring matching on bytes.
             ;; Let's use string-ref and char->integer to distinguish.
             ;; Actually simpler: check next 3 bytes.
             (let ((code (char->integer c)))
               ;; UTF-8 multi-byte: skip continuation bytes
               ;; ⊕ = U+2295 = E2 8A 95, ⊗ = U+2297 = E2 8A 97
               ;; We'll read 3 chars and dispatch on the 3rd.
               (if (and (= code #xE2) (< (+ i 2) (string-length s)))
                   (let ((c3 (char->integer (string-ref s (+ i 2)))))
                     (cond
                       ((= c3 #x95) (loop (+ i 3) (cons 'oplus toks)))
                       ((= c3 #x97) (loop (+ i 3) (cons 'otimes toks)))
                       (else (error "unknown op" c3))))
                   (loop (+ i 1) toks))))))))))

(define toks (tokenize expr))
(length toks)

;; Parse: expression is either a number, or (E OP E)
(define (parse toks)
  (let ((t (car toks)) (rest (cdr toks)))
    (cond
      ((number? t) (values t rest))
      ((eq? t 'lp)
       ;; parse E OP E then rp
       (call-with-values
         (lambda () (parse rest))
         (lambda (a r1)
           (let ((op (car r1)))
             (call-with-values
               (lambda () (parse (cdr r1)))
               (lambda (b r2)
                 ;; expect rp
                 (if (eq? (car r2) 'rp)
                     (let ((v (cond ((eq? op 'oplus) (min a b))
                                    ((eq? op 'otimes) (+ a b))
                                    (else (error "bad op" op)))))
                       (values v (cdr r2)))
                     (error "expected rp")))))))))))

(call-with-values (lambda () (parse toks))
  (lambda (v r) (list v r)))

(define (sym< a b) (string<? (symbol->string a) (symbol->string b)))
(define (dedup lst) (if (null? lst) '() (if (member (car lst) (cdr lst)) (dedup (cdr lst)) (cons (car lst) (dedup (cdr lst))))))
(define (U a b) (sort sym< (dedup (append a b))))
(define (I a b) (filter (lambda (x) (member x b)) a))

;; Inner intersection
(define LHS (I '(a d e i k l n) '(d f g i k l m n o p)))

;; Build the right-hand big expression
;; (({a,d,g} ∪ ({a,c,e,f,h,i,j,n,o} ∩ {e})) ∩ (((({b,c,g,h,i,j,l,n,o} ∪ {d,l}) ∩ {a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i,k,l,m,n,o,p}) ∪ ({a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i,j,k,l,n,o,p} ∩ {b,f,g,i,m})) ∩ ({b,c,d,e,f,g,k,l,m,n,o} ∪ {a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i,j,k,l,n,o,p})))
(define P1 (U '(a d g) (I '(a c e f h i j n o) '(e))))
(define P2a (I (U '(b c g h i j l n o) '(d l)) '(a b c d e f g h i k l m n o p)))
(define P2b (I '(a b c d e f g h i j k l n o p) '(b f g i m)))
(define P2 (U P2a P2b))
(define P3 (U '(b c d e f g k l m n o) '(a b c d e f g h i j k l n o p)))
(define P (I P1 (I P2 P3)))

;; second big union: 
;; ((({a,f,g,k,m,n,o} ∩ {a,b,c,d,e,i,j,k,l,m,n}) ∪ (({a,g,k,o,p} ∩ {c,d,e,f,g,i,l,o}) ∪ {a,c,e,f,g,i,k,l,m,n,p})) ∪ ((({i,k,n,o} ∪ {c,d,f}) ∪ ({f,k} ∩ {a,b,c,e,f,g,h,i,k,m,n,o,p})) ∪ {c,f,o,p}))
(define Q1 (I '(a f g k m n o) '(a b c d e i j k l m n)))
(define Q2 (U (I '(a g k o p) '(c d e f g i l o)) '(a c e f g i k l m n p)))
(define Q3 (U Q1 Q2))
(define Q4 (U (U '(i k n o) '(c d f)) (I '(f k) '(a b c e f g h i k m n o p))))
(define Q5 (U Q4 '(c f o p)))
(define Q (U Q3 Q5))

(define M (U P Q))
(define RESULT (I LHS M))
(list 'LHS LHS 'M M 'RESULT RESULT)

(sort LESS? LST) → list
  Form: procedure
  Return a list containing the elements of LST sorted according
  to the comparison procedure LESS?. The input list is not modified.
  Uses a stable merge sort: equal elements preserve their original
  order.
  Parameters:
    LESS? : procedure -- a two-argument comparison predicate
    LST : list
  Returns: list
  Category: lists
  Keywords: sort, order, merge sort, stable, list

;; Tokenize: tokens are { } ( ) , elem-letter, operators ∪ ∩
;; Let me convert the string to a Scheme-readable form
;; Replace { with (set , } with ), ∪ with U, ∩ with I
;; But the issue is the comma. Let me write a custom reader.

;; Approach: parse into AST.
;; Tokens: LBRACE, RBRACE, LPAREN, RPAREN, COMMA, UNION, INTER, LETTER

(define (tokenize s)
  (let loop ((i 0) (toks '()))
    (if (>= i (string-length s))
        (reverse toks)
        (let ((c (string-ref s i)))
          (cond
            ((char-whitespace? c) (loop (+ i 1) toks))
            ((char=? c #\{) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'LB toks)))
            ((char=? c #\}) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'RB toks)))
            ((char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'LP toks)))
            ((char=? c #\)) (loop (+ i 1) (cons 'RP toks)))
            ((char=? c #\,) (loop (+ i 1) toks))
            ((char-alphabetic? c) (loop (+ i 1) (cons c toks)))
            ;; ∪ in UTF-8: #xE2 #x88 #xAA = 226 136 170
            ;; ∩ in UTF-8: #xE2 #x88 #xA9 = 226 136 169
            ;; In Wile strings, char-ref returns chars. Multi-byte may be single chars or bytes depending.
            (else
             ;; assume non-ASCII multi-byte - check 3 bytes
             (let ((cc (char->integer c)))
               (cond
                 ((= cc 226)
                  (let ((c3 (char->integer (string-ref s (+ i 2)))))
                    (cond
                      ((= c3 170) (loop (+ i 3) (cons 'U toks)))
                      ((= c3 169) (loop (+ i 3) (cons 'I toks)))
                      (else (error "unknown utf8" c3)))))
                 (else (error "unknown char" cc)))))
            )))))

(define toks (tokenize "{a, b} ∪ {c}"))

;; Now a parser. Grammar:
;; expr := term (('U'|'I') term)*  — left associative, same precedence
;; term := '{' letters '}' | '(' expr ')'
;; Return value: sorted deduplicated list of chars.

(define tokens #f)
(define (peek) (if (null? tokens) #f (car tokens)))
(define (advance) (let ((t (car tokens))) (set! tokens (cdr tokens)) t))

(define (parse-term)
  (let ((t (advance)))
    (cond
      ((eq? t 'LB)
       (let loop ((acc '()))
         (let ((n (advance)))
           (cond
             ((eq? n 'RB) (sort char<? (delete-duplicates acc)))
             ((char? n) (loop (cons n acc)))
             (else (error "bad in set" n))))))
      ((eq? t 'LP)
       (let ((e (parse-expr)))
         (unless (eq? (advance) 'RP) (error "expected RP"))
         e))
      (else (error "bad term" t)))))

(define (parse-expr)
  (let loop ((left (parse-term)))
    (let ((p (peek)))
      (cond
        ((eq? p 'U) (advance) (loop (U left (parse-term))))
        ((eq? p 'I) (advance) (loop (I left (parse-term))))
        (else left)))))

(define (parse s)
  (set! tokens (tokenize s))
  (parse-expr))

(parse "{a, b} ∪ {c}")

(sort LESS? LST) → list
  Form: procedure
  Return a list containing the elements of LST sorted according
  to the comparison procedure LESS?. The input list is not modified.
  Uses a stable merge sort: equal elements preserve their original
  order.
  Parameters:
    LESS? : procedure -- a two-argument comparison predicate
    LST : list
  Returns: list
  Category: lists
  Keywords: sort, order, merge sort, stable, list

(import (srfi 1))
(define (U a b) (lset-union eq? a b))
(define (I a b) (lset-intersection eq? a b))
(define (sortit s) (sort (lambda (x y) (string<? (symbol->string x) (symbol->string y))) s))

;; Piece A1 = (((({g,j,m,n,p}∩{m})∩{n}) ∪ ({a,d,e,i,j,l,m,n} ∩ ({f,g,i,l,m,n}∩{b,c,d,e,g,h,i,j,k,l,m,p})))
(define A1
  (U (I (I '(g j m n p) '(m)) '(n))
     (I '(a d e i j l m n) (I '(f g i l m n) '(b c d e g h i j k l m p)))))
(sortit A1)

;; Piece A2 = ((((({d}∩{k})∩(...)) ∩ {a,d,e,f,k,l,m,p}) ∪ {d,g,k}) ∩ ({a,c,d,e,f,g,i,k,l,m,n,o,p}∩{b,k,l,n,p}))
(define A2-inner1 (I (I (I '(b c e f g j l o) '(b d e f h i j k n p)) '(a b c d f h j k l)) '(d h k)))
(define A2-a (I (I (I '(d) '(k)) A2-inner1) '(a d e f k l m p)))
(define A2-b (U A2-a '(d g k)))
(define A2-c (I '(a c d e f g i k l m n o p) '(b k l n p)))
(define A2 (I A2-b A2-c))
(sortit A2)

;; Piece A3 = (((({b,c,d,e,f,h,j,k,l,m,n,o,p}∪{a,e,g,j,l,m,n,o,p})∪{h}) ∪ ((({c,d,f,j,k,l,m,o,p}∪{a,b,c,d,e,f,h,i,j,k,m,n,p})∪({a,b,c,d,e,f,g,j,n,o,p}∩{a,b,d,e,f,g,h,i,j,k,l,m,n,o,p}))∩({c,d,m}∪{d,h,j,l}))) ∪ (({c,f,i,m}∩({a,b,d,f,g,h,k,l,n}∩{b,e,g,j,k,n}))∩({a,b,c,e,f,g,h,k,m,o,p}∪{a,c,i})))
(define A3-p1 (U (U (U '(b c d e f h j k l m n o p) '(a e g j l m n o p)) '(h))
                 (I (U (U '(c d f j k l m o p) '(a b c d e f h i j k m n p))
                       (I '(a b c d e f g j n o p) '(a b d e f g h i j k l m n o p)))
                    (U '(c d m) '(d h j l)))))
(define A3-p2 (I (I '(c f i m) (I '(a b d f g h k l n) '(b e g j k n)))
                 (U '(a b c e f g h k m o p) '(a c i))))
(define A3 (U A3-p1 A3-p2))
(sortit A3)

(sort LESS? LST) → list
  Form: procedure
  Return a list containing the elements of LST sorted according

  order.

;; Count open/close parens and find top level operators
(define (top-level-ops s)
  (let loop ((i 0) (depth 0) (ops '()))
    (if (>= i (string-length s))
        (reverse ops)
        (let ((c (string-ref s i)))
          (cond ((char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (+ depth 1) ops))
                ((char=? c #\)) (loop (+ i 1) (- depth 1) ops))
                ((and (= depth 1) (or (char=? c #\∩) (char=? c #\∪)))
                 (loop (+ i 1) depth (cons (cons c i) ops)))
                (else (loop (+ i 1) depth ops)))))))
(top-level-ops expr)

(define (top-level-ops-all s)
  (let loop ((i 0) (depth 0) (ops '()))
    (if (>= i (string-length s))
        (reverse ops)
        (let ((c (string-ref s i)))
          (cond ((char=? c #\() (loop (+ i 1) (+ depth 1) ops))
                ((char=? c #\)) (loop (+ i 1) (- depth 1) ops))
                ((and (= depth 0) (or (char=? c #\∩) (char=? c #\∪)))
                 (loop (+ i 1) depth (cons (cons c i) ops)))
                (else (loop (+ i 1) depth ops)))))))
(top-level-ops-all expr)
(string-length expr)
(top-level-ops-all expr)
